cross_scene_arcs.title: Interrogation Control and Escalation with Ammar
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Daniel systematically applies psychological and physical pressure on Ammar to extract intelligence, escalating from verbal dominance to waterboarding and public humiliation without immediate success.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daniel establishes authority over Ammar through threats and enforced eye contact.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ammar is stripped, chained, and subjected to waterboarding but remains uncooperative.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ammar continues to withhold actionable intelligence despite extreme coercion.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Shift to Rapport-Based Interrogation with Faraj
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After capturing Abu Faraj, Daniel shifts from overt coercion to rapport-building by offering music and casual engagement, marking a change in interrogation approach.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daniel observes Faraj's capture from a distance, establishing operational control.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daniel initiates a non-confrontational rapport by asking about Faraj's music preferences upon arrival at the black site.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Faraj's willingness to cooperate remains unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Mentorship and Divergence with Maya
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Daniel transitions from guiding Maya through interrogation work to clashing with her over analytical confidence, reflecting a shift from mentorship to professional divergence.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daniel acknowledges Maya's determination and supports her continued role in interrogations.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daniel expresses cautious skepticism about bin Laden's presence, leading to tension as Maya views him as undermining her certainty.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The rift between Daniel's experience-based caution and Maya's conviction remains unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Extraction of Intelligence on Abu Ahmed al-Kuwaiti
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Daniel secures a critical lead on al-Qaeda's network through Ammar's confession about Abu Ahmed al-Kuwaiti, later using implied threats to extract a contact in Kuwait.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ammar reveals Abu Ahmed delivered a letter from bin Laden but provides no identifying details.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daniel obtains a phone number from a Kuwaiti businessman by implying lethal consequences in Pakistan.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The reliability and ethical cost of targeting family members for intelligence remain unclear.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Withdrawal from Field Operations and Acceptance of Risk
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Daniel disengages from frontline interrogation, expressing personal fatigue, while simultaneously volunteering to take political responsibility for the detainee program.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daniel announces his departure from the black site, citing emotional exhaustion from prolonged exposure to detainees.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: He offers to be the public face of the controversial program, accepting potential fallout.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The personal and professional consequences of taking accountability are uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Tracking and Targeting Al-Qaeda Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jessica progresses from updating Al-Qaeda leadership assessments to leading a high-stakes operation to capture bin Laden through an informant, culminating in the fatal meeting with Balawi.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Abu Faraj is formally tracked as Al-Qaeda's number three based on Washington assessment
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: killed in suicide bombing during attempted engagement with a high-value source
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: the authenticity of the informant's access to bin Laden remains unverified

cross_scene_arcs.title: Evolving Relationship with Maya
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Begins with professional disagreement, shifts to strategic alignment, and deepens through shared trauma and personal gestures, forming a resilient working bond.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: adversarial dynamic established with Maya over threat assessment
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: reinforced personal and professional bond through shared survival and inclusion in team rituals

cross_scene_arcs.title: Operational Authority and Decision-Making Under Risk
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Jessica transitions from strategic advisor to mission commander, assuming increasing control over high-risk operations, ultimately making critical security decisions before the attack.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: positioned as voice of strategic continuity after intelligence failure
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: exerts authority to override security protocols moments before being killed in bombing
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: temporary suspension of security measures could expose the compound to risk

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict Over Intelligence Methodology and Trust
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Recurring tension emerges between institutional caution and operational urgency, particularly in assessing sources and handling detainees, reflecting broader methodological divides.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: disputes Maya's assertion about bin Laden's changed tactics, highlighting analytical divide
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: accepts need for face-to-face assessment but remains in conflict with Bradley over ethics and judgment
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: conflict between institutional experience and new operational perspectives

cross_scene_arcs.title: Relentless Pursuit of the Courier Lead
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Maya identifies Abu Ahmed al-Kuwaiti as a key courier to bin Laden and drives the intelligence effort to track him, culminating in the confirmation of bin Laden's location and the mission's success.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Maya extracts the name and role of Abu Ahmed al-Kuwaiti from Ammar during interrogation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Maya confirms the identity of bin Laden's body, closing the seven-year manhunt she helped initiate.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Moral and Emotional Toll of Interrogation Work
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Maya commits to aggressive interrogation methods despite visible distress, enduring psychological strain that culminates in a targeted assassination attempt, reflecting the personal cost of her role.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Maya insists on continuing the interrogation despite emotional discomfort.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Maya survives an ambush, revealing the extent of personal risk she faces.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Who ordered the attack and whether it was retaliation for her interrogation role

cross_scene_arcs.title: Emergence as a Credible and Authoritative Analyst
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Maya evolves from an overlooked analyst to the central authority on the bin Laden operation, with her assessments shaping high-level decisions and earning formal recognition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Maya offers a key insight about bin Laden's behavior that goes unacknowledged.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Maya's confirmation of bin Laden's identity is treated as definitive by military leadership.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Isolation and Emotional Release After Mission Completion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After years of solitary focus, Maya experiences a moment of emotional vulnerability when confronted with her singular importance and isolation on the return flight.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Maya completes the mission with quiet, resolute composure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Maya is emotionally overwhelmed upon hearing the pilot note she is the only passenger, signaling the end of her operational identity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: her destination and future role remain unknown
